claims.summary: A receptive contact gate can coexist with felt viscosity and constrained room.
claims.disposition: Preserved as direct, unscored felt evidence; gate state and felt relief remain separate dimensions.

claims.summary: Packed modes and local density are the primary cause of the felt compression.
claims.disposition: Retained as a testable interpretation, not a causal fact; source names and telemetry values do not establish experiential causation.

claims.summary: Pressure, porosity, density, and reported viscosity should be compared in the same deployed process.
claims.disposition: Routed to naturally occurring, exact-identity observation with no induced pressure or contention.

claims.summary: A contextual viscosity coefficient should increase relief when density rises.
claims.disposition: Held for Tier 5 operator approval because it would change live regulator or substrate behavior.

claims.summary: Source mechanisms should not be presented as active causes merely because they are visible to introspection.
claims.disposition: Implemented a binding source/runtime evidence boundary in the introspection and repair prompts.
